description_en: An intangible folk cultural property, a tori-mai (rooster dance) performing art. Village-designated by Tanohata Village on 1981-02-28.
A natural monument, the Chosen akashijimi (Korean hairstreak butterfly). Village-designated by Tanohata Village on 1973-07-14. Located throughout the village.
A natural monument, the Chosen akashijimi (Korean hairstreak butterfly). Village-designated by Fudai Village on 1984-10-01. Located along the village's rivers and riverbanks.

name_en: Married Couple Cedars of Unotori Shrine
description_en: A natural monument, a pair of cedar trees (meoto-sugi) at Unotori Shrine. Village-designated by Fudai Village on 1984-10-01. Located at Fudai Village 25-7.

name_en: Wooden Seated Amida Buddha in the Meditation Mudra
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the sculpture category, a wooden seated statue of Amida Nyorai in the jo-in (meditation) mudra. Town-designated by Sumita Town on 1963-07-01. Located in Kamiarisu.
A tangible cultural property in the sculpture category, a wooden statue of Shotoku Taishi (Prince Shotoku). Town-designated by Sumita Town on 1983-11-01. Located in Kamiarisu.
An intangible folk cultural property, a nenbutsu-kenbai (Buddhist sword dance). Town-designated by Sumita Town on 1973-11-01.
An intangible folk cultural property, a shishi-odori (deer dance) performing art. Town-designated by Sumita Town on 1973-11-01.
An intangible folk cultural property, a shishi-odori (deer dance) performing art. Town-designated by Sumita Town on 1973-11-01.
An intangible folk cultural property, a kagura sacred dance tradition. Town-designated by Sumita Town on 1976-07-17.
An intangible folk cultural property, a reenacted daimyo (feudal lord's) procession. Town-designated by Sumita Town on 1976-07-17.
An intangible folk cultural property, a kagura sacred dance tradition. Town-designated by Sumita Town on 1995-04-03.
